title: Probation Officer

rationales.taskAutomation: Risk-assessment scoring, case notes, and check-in scheduling are largely digital, but supervision decisions and the client relationship remain judgment-gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: Risk-assessment algorithms are already embedded in many probation departments' case-management systems and have been for years.
rationales.adoption: Courts and probation departments have adopted risk-assessment tools faster than most public-safety functions, though still trailing private-sector norms.
rationales.agenticExposure: Case-note generation and scheduling form an agent-ownable slice, while in-person supervision and relapse-risk judgment stay with the officer.

tasksAtRisk: Risk-assessment score generation and case notes
tasksGrowing: In-person supervision and relationship-building with clients
Judgment calls on treatment referrals and sanctions
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 21-1092, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Risk-assessment algorithm interpretation and case-management software

insight: Risk-scoring tools already do a chunk of the analytic work here; the supervisory relationship is what remains distinctly human.
